title: Significant: Variance additivity proof (Prop. prop:props (i)) is incomplete

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap Description

Proposition prop:props (i) claims: if gamma = Psi(alpha, beta), then
  Var(gamma) = Var(alpha) + Var(beta).

The proof says only "(i) follows from eq:walsh together with Newton identities applied
to the first two coefficients of p boxplus_n q." This is too brief.

## The Missing Computation

The first-moment part m_1(gamma) = m_1(alpha) + m_1(beta) is straightforward:
each permutation contributes sum_i(alpha_i + beta_{pi(i)}) = sum_i alpha_i + sum_j beta_j.

The SECOND moment requires computing the coefficient of x^{n-2} in the average:
  (1/n!) sum_pi e_2(alpha_1+beta_{pi(1)}, ..., alpha_n+beta_{pi(n)})

Expanding e_2 of sums:
  e_2(alpha_i + beta_{pi(i)} : i=1..n) = sum_{i<j} (alpha_i+beta_{pi(i)})(alpha_j+beta_{pi(j)})
  = sum_{i<j} [alpha_i*alpha_j + alpha_i*beta_{pi(j)} + beta_{pi(i)}*alpha_j + beta_{pi(i)}*beta_{pi(j)}]

The purely alpha/beta terms give e_2(alpha) + e_2(beta) after averaging.
The cross terms require:
  (1/n!) sum_pi sum_{i<j} [alpha_i * beta_{pi(j)} + alpha_j * beta_{pi(i)}]
  = (1/n!) sum_{i<j} (alpha_i + alpha_j) * sum_pi (beta_{pi(i)} + beta_{pi(j)})

For fixed i != j:  (1/n!) sum_pi beta_{pi(i)} = (1/n!) * (n-1)! * sum_k beta_k = (1/n) sum_k beta_k

So the cross terms give: (1/n) * sum_{i<j}(alpha_i+alpha_j) * 2 * sum_k beta_k
= (n-1)/n * (sum_i alpha_i)(sum_j beta_j)

Combined: the second coefficient of p boxplus_n q is
  e_2(alpha) + e_2(beta) + (n-1)/n * e_1(alpha)*e_1(beta)

From this one can verify m_2(gamma) - m_1(gamma)^2 = Var(alpha) + Var(beta), but
the detailed calculation is absent from the solution.

## Why This Matters

Variance additivity is essential for Lemma lem:hess (the Hessian identity), which in
turn underpins Proposition prop:contraction (the contraction estimate).
The identity partial_t^2 m_2(gamma(t))|_0 = partial_t^2 [m_2(alpha(t)) + m_2(beta(t))]|_0
is derived by differentiating Var(gamma(t)) = Var(alpha(t)) + Var(beta(t)), so
any gap here propagates to the heart of the proof.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution

I replaced the one-line proof of Proposition prop:props with a complete, self-contained derivation. The new proof establishes both coefficients explicitly from the symmetrized-product (Walsh) representation eq:walsh and then runs the variance algebra.

### What is now proved in full

**First coefficient.** For every permutation pi, e_1(gamma^pi) = sum_i(alpha_i + beta_{pi(i)}) = e_1(alpha)+e_1(beta), independent of pi, so c_1 = -(e_1(alpha)+e_1(beta)) and m_1(gamma)=m_1(alpha)+m_1(beta).

**Second coefficient.** Expanding e_2 of the sums splits into pure-alpha, pure-beta, and cross terms:
- pure-alpha term = e_2(alpha) (pi-independent);
- pure-beta term: since (pi(i),pi(j)) ranges over all unordered pairs as (i,j) does, it equals e_2(beta) (pi-independent);
- cross term averaged via the single-index identity (1/n!) sum_pi beta_{pi(i)} = (n-1)!/n! * sum_k beta_k = e_1(beta)/n, giving (1/n!) sum_pi (**) = sum_{i<j}(alpha_i+alpha_j) * e_1(beta)/n = (n-1)/n * e_1(alpha) e_1(beta), using sum_{i<j}(alpha_i+alpha_j) = (n-1) e_1(alpha).

Hence c_2 = e_2(alpha) + e_2(beta) + (n-1)/n * e_1(alpha) e_1(beta), exactly as the critic indicated.

**Variance.** Using Newton (n m_1 = e_1, n m_2 = e_1^2 - 2 e_2) so Var(v) = ((n-1)e_1(v)^2 - 2n e_2(v))/n^2, I substitute e_1(gamma)=A_1+B_1, e_2(gamma)=A_2+B_2+(n-1)/n A_1 B_1 and show the A_1 B_1 cross term cancels exactly: the coefficient is 2(n-1) - 2(n-1) = 0. The result is Var(gamma) = Var(alpha) + Var(beta).

I also expanded part (ii) (translation), which follows immediately from eq:walsh since shifting alpha by t shifts every factor x - gamma_i^pi to (x-t) - gamma_i^pi.

### Verification
- Symbolic check (sympy): with c_2 = e_2(alpha)+e_2(beta)+(n-1)/n e_1(alpha)e_1(beta) and the Newton formulas, Var(gamma)-(Var(alpha)+Var(beta)) simplifies to 0 identically in n.
- Numerical check: for random alpha,beta of degrees 2..5, computing p box_n q roots directly and comparing variances gives agreement to ~1e-13.

The gap is fully closed. (LaTeX compilation could not be run: no toolchain installed in the workspace; the begin/end proof environments are balanced and the edit is purely a textual replacement of the proof body.)
